feature: Inventory and dependency graph: manual incident creation / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on manual incident creation cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for manual incident creation: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover manual incident creation, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for manual incident creation.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for manual incident creation.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Incident Command Center manual incident creation inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: manual-incident-creation_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for manual incident creation cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for manual incident creation; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for manual incident creation.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for manual incident creation, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for manual incident creation.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for manual incident creation.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing manual incident creation data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling manual incident creation inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for manual incident creation.
acceptance_criteria: Given manual incident creation data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: manual incident creation connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for resource correlation: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Detect resource correlation symptoms, thresholds, anomalies, and correlated events that affect resilience.
backend_scope: Add deterministic evaluators, metrics ingestion, and explainable findings for resource correlation.
frontend_scope: Add health badges, charts, timelines, and affected-object drilldowns for resource correlation.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Incident Command Center resource correlation health with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: resource-correlation_resilience_health evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for resource correlation resilience that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for resource correlation; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for resource correlation.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for resource correlation recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for resource correlation.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for resource correlation.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing resource correlation data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling resource correlation health broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for resource correlation.
acceptance_criteria: Given resource correlation data exists, when the resilience health workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: resource correlation connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for resource correlation security posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for resource correlation.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for resource correlation.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Incident Command Center resource correlation automation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.

deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for resource correlation security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for resource correlation; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for resource correlation.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for resource correlation recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for resource correlation.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for resource correlation.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing resource correlation data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling resource correlation automation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for resource correlation.
acceptance_criteria: Given resource correlation data exists, when the security automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: resource correlation connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for symptom clustering: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Let an agent investigate symptom clustering operational-excellence issues with bounded tool use, read-only diagnostics first, explicit uncertainty, and approval-gated mutations.
backend_scope: Add tool registry, planner, execution trace, read-only default policy, token/time budgets, rollback plan schema, and stop conditions for symptom clustering.
frontend_scope: Add investigation timeline, tool-call evidence, confidence labels, approval prompts, replay, and promote-to-runbook actions for symptom clustering.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Incident Command Center symptom clustering agentic-investigation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: symptom-clustering_operational-excellence_agentic-investigation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for symptom clustering operational-excellence that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for symptom clustering; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for symptom clustering.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for symptom clustering recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for symptom clustering.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for symptom clustering.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing symptom clustering data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling symptom clustering agentic-investigation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for symptom clustering.
acceptance_criteria: Given symptom clustering data exists, when the operational-excellence agentic-investigation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: symptom clustering connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for customer impact estimate: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Detect customer impact estimate symptoms, thresholds, anomalies, and correlated events that affect scalability.
backend_scope: Add deterministic evaluators, metrics ingestion, and explainable findings for customer impact estimate.
frontend_scope: Add health badges, charts, timelines, and affected-object drilldowns for customer impact estimate.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Incident Command Center customer impact estimate health with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: customer-impact-estimate_scalability_health evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for customer impact estimate scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for customer impact estimate; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for customer impact estimate.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for customer impact estimate recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for customer impact estimate.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for customer impact estimate.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing customer impact estimate data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling customer impact estimate health broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for customer impact estimate.
acceptance_criteria: Given customer impact estimate data exists, when the scalability health workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: customer impact estimate connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for action timeline: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover action timeline, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the scalability pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for action timeline.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for action timeline.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Incident Command Center action timeline inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: action-timeline_scalability_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for action timeline scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for action timeline; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for action timeline.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for action timeline recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for action timeline.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for action timeline.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing action timeline data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling action timeline inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for action timeline.
acceptance_criteria: Given action timeline data exists, when the scalability inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: action timeline connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
